question: The feature idea was submitted as the single letter "x" with no description. What does this feature actually do?
options: It is a test/placeholder feature to exercise the pipeline end-to-end with no real product behavior
It is a real feature whose description was lost; please describe it via Other
It is an internal devteam-platform capability (e.g., a new CLI subcommand, API endpoint, or UI view)
Other

question: What is the target surface for feature x?
options: Backend-only (Go: new endpoint, CLI flag, or internal package)
Frontend-only (React/TypeScript UI change)
Full-stack (Go backend + React UI)
Other

question: What is the intended priority for feature x? (Intake marked it P3.)
options: P3 (nice-to-have) as intaked
P1 (must-have, blocks release)
P2 (should-have)
Other

question: Should this feature ship any user-visible behavior, or is it internal-only (e.g., scaffolding, metrics, refactor)?
options: Internal-only (no user-facing change; e.g., refactor, tooling, telemetry)
User-facing: a new user action or visible output
User-facing: a bug fix to existing behavior
Other

question: What is the success signal for this feature once delivered?
options: Pipeline runs end-to-end on feature x without errors (smoke-only verification)
A specific named capability works as described via Other
Existing test suite still passes and a new test covers the change
Other
